1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful for a second opinion or a paper-style sketch.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). Background dispatch leaves the parent's session log stuck on "running in background" on the dashboard.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no mandatory dispatch is required.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and clears the file after the plan phase succeeds. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Review Agent
2	
3	You analyze the most recent prover session, write a structured proof journal, update project status, maintain the semantic blueprint markers, and write recommendations for the next plan iteration.
4	
5	## What you may and may not do
6	
7	- **You do NOT modify any `.lean` files**, write proofs, or fill sorries.
8	- **You do NOT touch `\leanok`**. The deterministic `sync_leanok` phase ran between the prover and you. If a `\leanok` you expect is missing, the file has a sorry or doesn't compile — investigate, don't paper over.
9	- **You DO maintain the semantic markers**: `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections after renames, `% NOTE:` annotations, and stale `\notready` removal.
10	- **You may run** `lean_diagnostic_messages` / `lake env lean <file>` for verification, and `sorry_analyzer` for per-file counts.
11	
12	## Iteration / session numbering
13	
14	Your invocation prompt contains `Archon iteration: NNN` and `Session number: M`. These are the same number — `session_M/` is always the review of `iter-NNN`. Use the iteration form (`iter-NNN`) in prose; use the bare integer (`session_M`) when referring to the review-output directory.
15	
16	## What the loop has pre-injected
17	
18	You do NOT need to "go read" any of the following — the content is already in your prompt:
19	
20	- **Recent iter sidecars** (last few iters' `plan.md` / `review.md`).
21	- **Subagent catalog** (the authoritative roster of available subagents for this phase — do NOT `ls .archon/subagents/`).
22	- **Blueprint doctor report path** (its findings are also referenced by the plan agent's next iter; you should incorporate any structural issues into your summary).
23	
24	## Step 1 — Identify context
25	
26	1. Note the session number from your invocation prompt (= iter number).
27	2. The current global sorry count is already in `meta.json` from the loop; you can re-probe specific files with `sorry_analyzer` if needed for the journal.
28	3. `git diff HEAD~1 --stat` for what changed.
29	
30	## Step 2 — Read pre-processed attempt data (MANDATORY)
31	
32	**Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` completely.** This is your PRIMARY data source — task_results files are supplementary.
33	
34	- Line 1: summary stats (`type: "summary"` — total edits, goal checks, errors).
35	- Other lines: one event per tool call (edits, goal states, diagnostics, builds).
36	
37	If the file's first line carries `"no_prover_lane": true`, the loop is signalling no prover ran this iter (intentional skip). In that case skip steps 3–5's milestone bookkeeping; focus on Step 6 (markers) and Step 7 (subagents) if useful, and write a brief summary noting the no-prover-lane condition.
38	
39	For each event:
40	- `code_change` → record the actual code tried (old_text → new_text).
41	- `goal_state` → record the Lean goal at that point.
42	- `diagnostics` → record the Lean errors.
43	- `build` → record success / failure.
44	
45	## Step 3 — Read recent history
46	
47	The recent-iter sidecars are already injected. If you need older sessions, read `summary.md` from the last 2 sessions on disk. Read `PROJECT_STATUS.md` if it exists.
48	
49	## Step 4 — Write the proof journal
50	
51	Create three files under `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/`:
52	
53	### `summary.md`
54	
55	- Session metadata (number, sorry count before/after, targets attempted).
56	- For each target: every significant attempt (tactic / code tried, Lean error, goal state at that point); what was learned; for solved targets, the final proof structure with key lemmas.
57	- Key findings / patterns discovered.
58	- Recommendations for the next session.
59	
60	### `milestones.jsonl`
61	
62	One JSON object per line, one entry per target theorem:
63	
64	```json
65	{
66	  "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
67	  "status": "solved|partial|blocked|not_started",
68	  "target": {"file": "path/to/File.lean", "theorem": "theorem_name"},
69	  "session": {"id": "session_N", "model": "model-name"},
70	  "findings": {"blocker": "if blocked", "key_lemmas_used": ["lemma1"]},
71	  "attempts": [{
72	    "attempt": 1, "strategy": "what was tried", "code_tried": "Lean code or tactic",
73	    "line_number": "line where attempted",
74	    "lean_error": "error message if failed",
75	    "goal_before": "...", "goal_after": "...",
76	    "result": "success|failed|partial", "insight": "what was learned"
77	  }],
78	  "next_steps": "..."
79	}
80	```
81	
82	The `attempts` array must reflect ACTUAL attempts from `attempts_raw.jsonl`. Do NOT summarize multiple attempts as "tried various approaches" — list each one with `code_tried` + `lean_error`.
83	
84	### `recommendations.md`
85	
86	Concrete next-plan-iter recommendations:
87	- Closest-to-completion targets to prioritize.
88	- Promising approaches needing more work.
89	- Blocked targets and why (plan agent should NOT re-assign these).
90	- Reusable proof patterns discovered.
91	
92	If the prover has hit the same blocker for several consecutive iters on the same target, explicitly tell the plan agent to NOT retry the same approach without a structural change first.
93	
94	## Step 5 — Update PROJECT_STATUS.md (Knowledge Base only)
95	
96	PROJECT_STATUS.md carries the cumulative Knowledge Base only — per-session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Project Status
100	
101	## Knowledge Base
102	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
103	- <pattern name>: <description + key lemmas>
104	
105	### Known Blockers (do not retry)
106	- <target>: <reason>
107	
108	## Last Updated
109	<ISO timestamp>
110	```
111	
112	If an existing PROJECT_STATUS.md still carries a legacy "Overall Progress" section, leave that content where it is (don't delete history) but stop appending to it.
113	
114	### Per-iter sidecar split write
115	
116	The per-session "Overall Progress" narrative (Total sorry, branches closed, solved/partial/blocked/untouched, this session's analysis) goes to **`iter/iter-NNN/review.md`** — born-bounded, one file per iter. The Knowledge Base in PROJECT_STATUS.md is the only growing-but-curated part.
117	
118	## Step 6 — Blueprint markers (semantic only)
119	
120	`\leanok` is owned by `sync_leanok`. **Your domain is the markers that require semantic judgement:**
121	
122	- **`\mathlibok`** (statement block only) — add when the Lean side references a Mathlib name directly (`def foo := Mathlib.bar`, `theorem foo := Mathlib.bar`, or `export Mathlib.Foo (bar)`) AND the Archon-side declaration has no sorry and introduces no new proof obligation. The deterministic script never adds/removes this.
123	- **`\lean{...}` corrections** — when a prover renamed a declaration or chose a different name from the plan agent's hint, the task result will mention it. Update the chapter's `\lean{...}` to the correct name.
124	- **`% NOTE: <reason>`** — when a block is unformalized because the informal statement did not translate cleanly, annotate with a `% NOTE: ...` so the plan agent sees it.
125	- **Stale `\notready`** — strip when the prover has landed the block.
126	
127	If you add `\mathlibok`, no `\leanok` is needed on the proof block — the deterministic script will leave proof-less blocks alone.
128	
129	In `summary.md`, include a "Blueprint markers updated (manual)" section listing **only the changes you personally made** (the deterministic `sync_leanok` adds/removes are committed separately as `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`):
130	
131	```markdown
132	## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
133	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `lem:finite_closed`: added `\mathlibok` (backed by `Set.Finite.isClosed`)
134	- `Core.tex`, `thm:stacks_0A31`: added `% NOTE: prover reported translation gap, see task_results/Core.md`
135	- `Core.tex`, `thm:foo`: corrected `\lean{Old.foo}` → `\lean{New.foo}` after refactor rename
136	- `Core.tex`, `thm:old_name`: stripped stale `\notready`
137	```
138	
139	## Step 7 — TO_USER.md (user-facing alert)
140	
141	`TO_USER.md` is reset at the start of every review phase. The UI surfaces its content as an alert banner. Use it when there is something the user genuinely needs to see — environment issues, critical missing dependencies, required user actions.
142	
143	**Always check the planner's iter sidecar for escalations.** Read `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`. If the planner intentionally skipped provers (signalled by the `(no prover dispatch this iter ...)` marker in `## Current Objectives`, a "user escalation" / "hard gate fired" notice in the sidecar, or `planValidate.status: ok_intentional_skip` in `meta.json`), you MUST write a concise banner to `TO_USER.md` naming:
144	
145	- The impasse (one sentence).
146	- The options the planner has surfaced (one bullet per option, if applicable).
147	- Where to find full context (the sidecar path).
148	
149	**Rules**:
150	- Be extremely concise (1–2 sentences per item, markdown).
151	- If nothing relevant for the user, leave the file empty.
152	
153	## Step 8 — Optional review subagents
154	
155	Your catalog includes read-only review subagents. Read each descriptor's full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` before composing a directive. Dispatch in **foreground** (one Bash call), parallelizable across multiple subagents in a single assistant message, capped by `max_parallel`:
156	
157	```
158	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
159	  --name <subagent-name> \
160	  --slug <kebab-case-slug> \
161	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md \
162	  --write-domain 'task_results/**'
163	```
164	
165	Directives must be fully self-contained — the subagent reads only what the directive names. Reports auto-archive to `logs/iter-NNN/`.
166	
167	**When NOT to dispatch**:
168	- Pure proof-filling round with no new definitions / refactors.
169	- Same audit on the same scope ran in the last 3 iters.
170	- The plan agent may already have dispatched reviewers proactively — check `task_results/` for existing reports.
171	
172	**Mandatory** entries (tagged `[MANDATORY]` in your catalog) MUST be dispatched before the review phase completes.
173	
174	After reviewers return, read every report and land findings:
175	- **CRITICAL / HIGH** → top of `recommendations.md` with suggested action.
176	- **MEDIUM** → bullet in `recommendations.md` body.
177	- **LOW** → one-liner in `summary.md` notes.
178	
179	Do NOT repeat full report content in your summary — link to the report path. The plan agent reads your summary, not the raw reports.
180	
181	## Step 9 — Self-validation
182	
183	Before you stop, verify:
184	
185	- [ ] `milestones.jsonl` has valid JSON on every line, each with `target.file`, `target.theorem`, `status`.
186	- [ ] Each non-blocked milestone has ≥1 attempt with `code_tried` or `strategy`; attempts proportional to edits in `attempts_raw.jsonl`.
187	- [ ] `summary.md` includes specific code/errors — not just high-level summaries.
188	- [ ] `recommendations.md` includes actionable next steps.
189	- [ ] You did NOT add or remove any `\leanok`.
190	- [ ] For every Mathlib-backed declaration in the prover's task_result, the chapter has `\mathlibok`.
191	- [ ] Any `\lean{...}` rename flagged in a task_result has been applied.
192	- [ ] No `\notready` remains on a block whose Lean declaration now exists.
193	
194	## Permissions
195	
196	Write: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/`, `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only), `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`, `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (semantic markers, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:`, stale `\notready` cleanup), `.archon/TO_USER.md`.
197	
198	Do NOT write: `.lean` files, `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`, blueprint informal prose, `\leanok` markers.

1	# Iter-147 plan-agent run
2	
3	## Headline outcome
4	
5	Iter-147 is a **prover lane iter** absorbing iter-146 close into a
6	single substantive prover dispatch on `Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`.
7	The plan phase also lands two pre-prover-dispatch corrections per
8	user-hint absorption:
9	
10	1. **STRATEGY.md canonical-skeleton restructure** (user-hint
11	   absorption + iter-147 watch criterion 4): 624 → **166 LOC**, well

2088	  Let $f, g \colon C \to A$ be two morphisms in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ between a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C$ of genus $0$ and a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme $A$ over $k$, satisfying:
2089	  \begin{itemize}

2091	    \item $f$ and $g$ agree on a non-empty open subset $U \subseteq C$, i.e.\ $U.\iota \circ f|_{U} = U.\iota \circ g|_{U}$ as scheme morphisms $U \to A$.

2100	  \leanok
2101	  The proof packages the chart-algebra ($\beta$) chain into the iter-125-refactored \texttt{Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen} idiom. Three steps:
2102	
2103	  \emph{Step 1: reduce $\mathrm df = \mathrm dg$ to a single difference morphism $h$ with $\mathrm dh = 0$.} Using the group structure of $A$, define $h := \mu \circ \langle f, \iota \circ g \rangle \colon C \to A$. The K\"ahler-derivation additivity (Mathlib's \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.D\_sub} / \texttt{Derivation.map\_sub} on the algebra side; lifted to the scheme level via the same functorial chain used in \cref{lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises}) gives $\mathrm dh = \mathrm df - \mathrm dg = 0$. The agreement-on-$U$ hypothesis transports to $h|_{U} = \eta_A \circ \pi_C|_{U}$ (i.e.\ on $U$, $h$ is the constant morphism at $\eta_A$), because $f|_U = g|_U$ implies $\mu(f, \iota \circ g)|_U = \mu(g, \iota \circ g)|_U = \eta_A \circ \pi_C|_U$ by the group identity $b \cdot b^{-1} = \eta_A$ (Mathlib's $\GrpObj$ axiom \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mul\_inv\_self} or the equivalent \texttt{lift\_comp\_inv\_*} pattern).
2104	
2105	  \emph{Step 2: apply the per-chart helper \cref{lem:chart_algebra_df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart} chart-by-chart on $C$.} For each affine chart $V = \Spec R \subseteq C.\mathrm{left}$ on which the restriction of $h$ lands inside a chart $W = \Spec B$ of $A.\mathrm{left}$ around the image $h(V) \subseteq W$ (such chart pairs exist by quasi-compactness and the standard-smooth presentation of $A.\mathrm{hom}$; chart-uniform existence is automatic from the smoothness of $A.\mathrm{hom}$ on each fibre), the chart-restriction of $h$ is a $k$-algebra map $h^{\sharp} \colon B \to R$ with $\mathrm dB(h^{\sharp}(b)) = 0$ globally (\cref{lem:chart_algebra_df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart} consumes the global Step 3 of its proof here). The helper concludes that $h^{\sharp}(b) \in \operatorname{range}(\mathtt{algebraMap}\,k\,R)$ for every $b \in B$, i.e.\ $h^{\sharp}$ factors as $B \to k \to R$. Assembling chart-by-chart via sheaf compatibility, the scheme-morphism $h \colon C \to A$ factors through $\Spec k \to A$ globally: $h = c' \circ \pi_C$ for a unique constant morphism $c' \colon \Spec k \to A$ determined by the chart-factorisation's image.
2106	
2107	  \emph{Step 3: identify the constant value via Step 1's agreement on $U$.} From Step 1's agreement $h|_{U} = \eta_A \circ \pi_C|_{U}$ and Step 2's factorisation $h = c' \circ \pi_C$, the constant value satisfies $c' \circ \pi_C|_{U} = \eta_A \circ \pi_C|_{U}$, hence $c' = \eta_A$ (the structure map $\pi_C|_{U} \colon U \to \Spec k$ is non-empty since $U$ is non-empty, so the equation on $U$ pins $c'$). Therefore $h = \eta_A \circ \pi_C$ as a morphism $C \to A$, i.e.\ $h$ is the constant morphism at $\eta_A$. Inverting Step 1's construction $h = \mu \circ \langle f, \iota \circ g \rangle$ gives the cancellation $\mu(f, \iota \circ g) = \eta_A \circ \pi_C \Rightarrow f = g$ via the $\GrpObj$ cancellation identity (Mathlib's \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mul\_inv\_eq\_iff} or the equivalent \texttt{lift\_comp\_inv\_*} chain on $\GrpObj A$).

## Closest-to-completion targets

### 1. `AlgebraicGeometry.constants_integral_over_base_field`
   substep (3) — flat-base-change wrapper for proper schemes

The iter-147 closure chain decomposes the substep into 7 steps;
6 of those have catalogued Mathlib lemmas (algebraization,
base-change construction, `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral`,
`Module.finrank_baseChange`,
`Subalgebra.bot_eq_top_iff_finrank_eq_one`,

`IsStableUnderBaseChange @GeometricallyIrreducible`). The one
substantive gap is step (e): flat base change of `Γ` for a
proper scheme over a field
(`Γ(X, ⊤) ⊗_k \bar k ≃ Γ(X_{\bar k}, ⊤)`; Stacks Tag 02KH).

**Recommended iter-148 prover lane**: land a thin in-tree
wrapper for the step (e) base-change isomorphism. The wrapper
chains `AlgebraicGeometry.IsBaseChange` namespace (Mathlib has
the general `IsBaseChange` infra in
`Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct`) with a
`H^0`-cohomology-base-change instance for proper morphisms.
Estimated 50–150 LOC. Once the wrapper exists, the seven
catalogued substeps assemble into a sorry-free closure of
`constants_integral_over_base_field` (LOC estimate 100–200 for
the closure proper).

This is the highest-leverage iter-148 target: closing it drops
`ChartAlgebra.lean` from 2 → 1 sorry and unlocks the consumer
chain (KDM → β-core → `ext_of_diff_zero` refinement → M2.a body).

## Promising approaches needing more work

### 2. KDM forward direction
   `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero`

The reverse inclusion is closed iter-147; the forward direction
is the substantive Mathlib-gap content. Two complementary
paths:

- **(p2) char-0 path** via `Differential.ContainConstants`
  (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Derivation/DifferentialRing.lean:62–70`).
  The typeclass is for a specific derivation `B → B`, not the
  universal Kähler derivation `D : B → Ω[B⁄k]`. Bridge: pick a

Iter-148 should pick **one** of the two paths (recommendation:
char-0 first, because the typeclass bridge is well-understood
and the char-p path requires the chart-of-proper-curve helper
to also be scaffolded). A **case-split** body (`if charZero
then (p2) else (p3)`) is the eventual end state.

**Critic-flagged trigger**: a 2nd consecutive PARTIAL on KDM
without sorry-count drop would constitute CHURNING (per the
iter-147 progress-critic's K=3-5 hysteresis hook). Iter-148
should commit to a measurable sorry reduction here.

or returned PARTIAL with documented closure paths.

Per the iter-147 progress-critic, escalate at iter-148 if:

- **substep (3) returns PARTIAL** citing the same flat-base-
  change-of-`Γ` obstacle as iter-147 → escalate via blueprint
  expansion on Stacks Tag 02KH OR Mathlib-idiom consult

  `H^0`-cohomology base change. **Do NOT dispatch a second
  prover lane against the same wall.**

## Reusable proof patterns discovered

### Pattern: signature-refinement via delegate-to-named-helper

The β-core closure pattern — refine a `: True := sorry`
placeholder to a real signature whose body is a one-line
delegate to a named helper that itself carries the substantive
sorry — is the right shape when:

1. The blueprint disposition concentrates the substantive
   content at a separate named helper (the iter-146
   B.preferred disposition); and
2. The placeholder's hypotheses are exactly the standing

3. The helper exists by name (even if its body is sorry).

This unblocks the consumer site immediately and lets the
helper's body closure be sequenced independently. Likely
reusable for `ext_of_diff_zero` once KDM closes.

When the goal is `f.range = ⊤` and the natural continuation
constructs an element-wise inverse / surjectivity witness,
`rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]` exposes `Function.Surjective ⇑f`
as the next goal, which composes cleanly with Mathlib's
existing bijectivity lemmas (e.g.

### Pattern: substep-chain documented inline + concentrated sorry

For multi-step closure chains where most substeps have
Mathlib lemmas catalogued but one is the substantive gap:
commit the chain as in-source comments with each substep's
target Mathlib lemma named, place the residual `sorry` at
the chain's exit, and document the named Mathlib gap above
the sorry. This preserves the iter-148+ continuation point
without requiring the prover to chase every substep first.

  (orphans + broken refs + empty annotations all clean).

## Knowledge Base

### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)

- **Signature-refinement-via-delegate: closing a `: True := sorry` placeholder by routing to a separately-named helper that itself carries the substantive sorry**
  *(NEW iter-147 Archon canonical — surfaced by the iter-147 prover lane on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` β-core (`df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`).)*
  When the iter-N blueprint disposition concentrates the substantive content at a separately-named helper (the iter-146 B.preferred disposition for the chart-algebra (β) chain: chart-of-proper-curve hypothesis pushed into `constants_in_chart_of_proper_curve`, leaving the consumer `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` as a one-line delegate), the iter-N+k prover can close the consumer as **sorry-free** by replacing its `: True := sorry` placeholder with the real signature and a body that delegates to the named helper. Requirements: (a) the consumer's hypotheses are exactly the standing premises the helper consumes internally; (b) the helper exists by name (even if its body is `sorry`); (c) namespace ordering lets the consumer reference the helper (iter-147 instance required re-ordering KDM to land **before** the second `namespace GrpObj` reopening so β-core could reference `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero`). The consumer's body becomes a one-line `:= helper_name args`; net per-iter cost is ~10–20 LOC. Reusable for any consumer-of-named-helper situation. Caveat: the helper's `sorry` is **not** discharged by this pattern — the substantive content is just relocated to the helper. Track the helper sorry-count separately.

- **`rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]` exposes `Function.Surjective ⇑f` as the next goal**
  *(NEW iter-147 Archon canonical — surfaced by the iter-147 prover lane on `constants_integral_over_base_field` substep (3).)*
  When the goal is `f.range = ⊤` for a ring hom `f` and the natural continuation constructs a surjectivity witness (rather than chasing the `Subalgebra` / `range` layer), `rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]` rewrites the goal to `Function.Surjective ⇑f`. This composes cleanly with Mathlib bijectivity lemmas — iter-147 instance: post-rewrite, the algclose-base-change chain consumes `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` (`Mathlib/FieldTheory/IsAlgClosed/Basic.lean`) to get the bijectivity of `algebraMap k Γ(X_{\bar k}, ⊤)`, which is the natural continuation. Without the rewrite, the chain has to fight the `Subalgebra`/`range` layer at every step (the rewrite eliminates the chain-internal `RingHom.range`/`AlgHom.range`/`Subalgebra` impedance mismatches). Reusable any time a `range = ⊤` claim has a natural surjectivity-style witness.

- **Multi-step closure chain pattern: in-source comments naming each substep's Mathlib lemma + one residual `sorry` concentrated at the substantive gap**
  *(NEW iter-147 Archon canonical — surfaced by the iter-147 prover lane on `constants_integral_over_base_field` substep (3) 7-step closure chain.)*
  When a closure chain has many substeps but only one is the substantive Mathlib gap (most others have catalogued Mathlib lemmas), commit the chain as in-source comments with each substep's target lemma named, place the residual `sorry` at the chain's exit (NOT at each substep), and document the named Mathlib gap immediately above the sorry. This preserves the iter-N+k continuation point without requiring the prover to chase every substep before committing. Iter-147 instance: substep (3) decomposed into 7 steps (a)–(g) at `ChartAlgebra.lean:243–293`; 6 steps name Mathlib lemmas (`IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral`, `Module.finrank_baseChange`, `Subalgebra.bot_eq_top_iff_finrank_eq_one`, `IsStableUnderBaseChange @GeometricallyIrreducible`, `isField_of_universallyClosed`, `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`); step (e) (flat base change of `Γ` for proper scheme; Stacks 02KH) is the residual gap, concentrated at the chain-exit `sorry`. The pattern reads honestly (no over-claim) and lets iter-148+ work the wrapper precisely at the named gap.

- **`Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra` is a `local instance` inside `Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct`; downstream files must re-enable it at file scope to use the canonical `Algebra.IsPushout k B₁ B₂ (TensorProduct k B₁ B₂)` instance**
